vocabulary.name: Bing News Search API Vocabulary
vocabulary.description: Domain terms and concepts used by the Bing News Search API v7.

terms.definition: The top-level response object returned by the /news/search and /news endpoints when the request succeeds.
The top-level response object returned by the /news/trendingtopics endpoint, containing a list of currently trending news topics on social networks.
A single news article returned in a news search or category response, including title, URL, description, publication date, image thumbnail, and provider metadata.
A single trending news topic including its name, query term, related search URLs, and an optional image.
Defines the news provider (publisher) that ran or owns a news article or image.
A thumbnail image associated with a news article or trending topic.
A link to a thumbnail image, including its URL and dimensions in pixels.
The width and height dimensions of media content such as video thumbnails.
A video related to a news article, including embed HTML, motion thumbnail, and thumbnail URL.
A search query string used to retrieve a trending topic.
A list of news articles related to the original search query, paired with a query term and web search URL.
An option for sorting news search results, either by relevance or by date.
A contractual rule requiring plain text attribution to be displayed with a news article.
Describes an individual error that occurred in processing a request.
The top-level object returned when a request fails, containing a list of Error objects.
A query parameter that filters news articles by age: Day (last 24 hours), Week (last 7 days), or Month (last 30 days). Only applicable to /news/search.
A query parameter controlling adult content filtering. Values: Off, Moderate (default), Strict.
The market where results come from, in the form <language>-<country> (e.g., en-US). Mutually exclusive with the cc parameter.
A 2-character country code that, combined with the Accept-Language header, determines the market. Mutually exclusive with the mkt parameter.
A query parameter for the /news endpoint specifying the category of news to return (e.g., Sports, Business, Entertainment, Health, ScienceAndTechnology, Politics, World). Categories vary by market.
A UNIX epoch timestamp used with the /news/trendingtopics endpoint to return only topics discovered on or after that time. Requires sortBy=Date.
When true, news article Image objects include the contentUrl pointing to the original image on the publisher website. When false, only the thumbnail is included.
When true, display strings may include hit highlighting markers. Used with the textFormat parameter.
The type of markers used for text decorations: Raw (Unicode) or HTML (HTML tags).
The required request header containing the Azure subscription key for authenticating Bing News Search API requests.
An optional request/response header that Bing uses to provide consistent behavior across API calls for a specific user on a specific device. Should be persisted and reused across sessions.
An optional request header containing the IPv4 or IPv6 address of the client device, used to discover the user's location for safe search and local relevance.
An optional request header containing semicolon-delimited key/value pairs describing the client's geographical location (lat, long, re, ts, etc.).
A response header containing the market used by the request, in <languageCode>-<countryCode> form.
A response header containing the ID of the log entry for the request. Useful for debugging and support.
A boolean field on NewsArticle that indicates whether the article is a headline. Only included in category requests that do not specify a specific category.
A boolean field on Topic that indicates whether a trending topic qualifies as breaking news.
The estimated total number of news articles matching the search query. Used with count and offset for pagination.
A list of rules on NewsArticle that must be followed when displaying the article. May include TextAttribution rules. Only present in Web Search API responses.
